issues.summary: [GH#162] VALKEY_HOST singleton-guard: early return leaves orphaned client running
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/162

Problem:
In server/valkey.ts, createValkeyClient() returns null early when VALKEY_HOST is unset, even if a client was previously created with that variable set. This leaves the existing client (and any refresh timer) running, creating a resource leak.

The documented behavior says an existing healthy client should be returned immediately — the implementation contradicts this.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Call createValkeyClient() with VALKEY_HOST set
2. Unset VALKEY_HOST
3. Call createValkeyClient() again — returns null but existing client still runs

Fix options:
- Clean up the existing client before the early return (call shutdownValkey() first)
- OR update the docs to reflect actual behavior

Priority: P1 — labeled by original reviewer
Affects: Rate limiter distributed state across Express replicas

issues.summary: [GH#163] Catch quit() failures before reinitializing Valkey client
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/163

Problem:
In server/valkey.ts, when the singleton guard detects an unhealthy client, it calls shutdownValkey() which attempts client.quit(). If client.quit() fails (common with broken sockets), the rejection prevents reinitialization and recovery.

Result: transient Redis/Valkey failures escalate into hard failures instead of recovering gracefully.

Expected behavior:
If client.quit() throws/rejects, the reinit should proceed anyway — the old broken connection should be discarded and a fresh client created.

Fix:
Wrap client.quit() in try/catch (or .catch(() => {})) so that quit failures are swallowed and reinitialization continues.

Priority: P1 — flagged in code review
Affects: Rate limiter resilience; impacts KAN-17 in legacy tracking

issues.summary: [GH#103] Pytest failing — Flask context error in generate_ai_image()
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegood.com/issues/103

Repo: Backend submodule (tasteslikegood.com)

Problem:
Tests fail because generate_ai_image() attempts to access Flask request-bound objects (session, request context) without an active application context.

Error: RuntimeError: Working outside of request context

Root cause:
The function accesses Flask's session or request objects directly without first checking if a request context is active.

Fix:
Guard session access with flask.has_request_context() checks, e.g.:
python
if flask.has_request_context():
    user_credentials = session.get('credentials')
else:
    user_credentials = None


Labels: bug, python, CI/CD
Affects: CI pipeline — tests fail on PR, blocking merges

issues.summary: Phase 0 — Pre-work: Data Prep & Content Definition
issues.description: All foundational work that must be completed before writing a single line of Godot code. Includes exporting the agent directory to JSON, defining open content items (assistant name, company name, art direction), and establishing data formats.

issues.summary: Phase 1 — Godot 4 Prototype (Weeks 1–3)
issues.description: Build the 3D office world with first-person navigation, NPC proximity-dialogue, the agent data layer, assistant chat UI, and the unlock gate system. Critical path: M1 → M4 → M8. If you can walk through a lobby, approach an NPC, and see their agent info — Phase 1 is proven.

issues.summary: Phase 2 — I/O Bridge: Live Agent Output (Weeks 4–6)
issues.description: Connect the Godot game world to real agent CLI processes via a local Python WebSocket bridge. M8 is the proof-of-concept milestone: walk up to an NPC → type a question → real agent response appears in the dialogue panel. The game becomes the tool.

issues.summary: Phase 3 — Polish, All 9 Floors & Full Tutorial Arc
issues.description: Post-prototype expansion: all 9 department floors, full NPC set, NPC animations + pathfinding, complete Week 1 tutorial arc, real assistant API connection, streaming I/O visualization, art pass, ambient sound. Define specifics once M8 is working.

issues.summary: Define in-world assistant — name and personality
issues.description: The always-on navigation companion that appears as a chat overlay (not an agent NPC). Warm, slightly playful tone. Separate from the employee characters — this is the player's guide to the office world itself. Name and personality should feel intentional, not generic. Worth designing carefully before scripting any dialogue.

issues.summary: Define tutorial startup — company name and backstory
issues.description: The fictional startup the player has been building with the founder before Day 0. Should feel like a real startup — not a placeholder. Name, what the product does, how far along it is. This is the emotional context for why the new office matters. The company name and backstory will appear in Founder call dialogue (Day 0) and throughout the tutorial arc.

issues.summary: Export 137 agent .md files to agents.json
issues.description: Convert the full employee directory from .md format to a single agents.json file. Required before Milestone 3 (AgentRegistry autoload). Schema per agent: { name, role, dept, color, description, tools }. Verify all 137 entries parse correctly and the structure matches the planned AgentRegistry.gd loader. Source: adamtasteslikegood/claude-code-tresor.

issues.summary: Define art direction and rough visual moodboard
issues.description: Even a rough direction helps grey-boxing decisions. Questions to answer: interior style (modern tech-office? brutalist? warm indie?), lighting mood (warm and lived-in or cool and corporate?), color palette consistency with the 9 department color codes. One or two reference images / screenshots is enough to unblock M2.

issues.summary: M1 — Install Godot 4 + first-person controller (~1 hour)
issues.description: Download Godot 4 from godotengine.org (~80MB, no install needed). Open Asset Library inside Godot → search "First Person" → download the FPS demo. Hit Play and walk around. That working FPS controller is your foundation. Create a new project and copy the CharacterBody3D controller into it. Done when: player walks, looks around, has collision.

issues.summary: M2 — Grey-box the office layout (4–6 hours)
issues.description: Build the entire office space with plain CSGBox3D geometry before any textures or art. Rooms to block out: lobby/entrance, player's office, server room, engineering floor (open plan), war room/meeting room, 2–3 locked corridors. Add Area3D doorway triggers on each room entrance. Bake NavigationRegion3D for pathfinding. Done when: you can walk through the whole space and it feels right to navigate. Do NOT add art until this is validated.

issues.summary: M3 — Agent data layer: load employee directory as game data (~2 hours)
issues.description: Convert agents.json (from Phase 0) into the game's data layer. Create autoload/AgentRegistry.gd that loads agents.json at startup and returns agent data by ID. Create autoload/GameEvents.gd with core signals: npc_approached, npc_left, task_completed, floor_unlocked. Create autoload/GameState.gd with unlock tracking. Add all three to Project → Autoload. Done when: AgentRegistry.get_agent("systems-architect") returns correct data in-game.

issues.summary: M4 — First NPC + proximity dialogue (~3 hours) [CRITICAL PATH]
issues.description: The core loop. NPC scene: CharacterBody3D + Area3D proximity trigger + Label3D name tag. npc.gd reads from AgentRegistry by agent_id export var. When player enters proximity → GameEvents.npc_approached fires → HUD dialogue panel populates from agent data. First NPC: Systems Architect in the server room. Done when: walk up → panel shows agent name + role + description. Walk away → panel hides. This template is used for all future NPCs.

issues.summary: M5 — Assistant chat UI overlay (~4 hours)
issues.description: Always-on CanvasLayer chat panel (NOT an NPC). Toggle with Tab or tilde key. Contains: scrollable message history, LineEdit input, Send button. Returns scripted/hardcoded responses in Phase 1. This is the player's always-on companion separate from the agent NPCs — it handles navigation, schedule, and task check-ins. Phase 2 upgrade: swap scripted responses for HTTP/WebSocket calls to real API.

issues.summary: M6 — Unlock gate system + 2D building map (~5 hours)
issues.description: GameState.complete_task() checks unlock gate rules and calls unlock_floor(). Door triggers check GameState.is_unlocked(). 2D building map widget in HUD: rooms are colored rects using department colors, locked rooms grey, updates on GameEvents.floor_unlocked. Default unlocked: lobby + server room. First unlock gate: complete first sprint task → engineering floor unlocks. Done when: completing a mock task unlocks a door and the map updates.

issues.summary: M7 — Python WebSocket bridge (~1 day)
issues.description: A small Python process running alongside the game on the same machine. Godot sends task requests over local WebSocket (ws://localhost:8765); bridge spawns agent CLI subprocess, captures stdout, returns result as JSON. No cloud, no deployment, no auth. Install: pip install websockets. Test standalone with wscat before wiring into Godot. Then write ws_client.gd in Godot and test the full round-trip: Godot sends → bridge receives (log) → bridge returns → Godot receives (log).

issues.summary: M8 — First live agent output in-world [PROTOTYPE COMPLETE] (2–4 hours)
issues.description: Wire it all together: walk up to Systems Architect NPC → dialogue panel opens → player types a real question → ws_client.send_task() fires → bridge invokes @systems-architect via CLI → response JSON returns to Godot → output appears in dialogue panel → response also logged to inbox. This is the milestone where the game becomes the tool. Everything after this point is polish and expansion.

issues.summary: Phase 3 planning — define specifics once M8 is working
issues.description: Post-M8 expansion work. Rough scope: all 9 department floors fully blocked out and textured, full NPC set (all dept leads + core 8 agents), NPC idle animations + pathfinding, complete Day 0–5 tutorial arc with full dialogue scripts, real assistant API connection (replace scripted responses), streaming I/O output visualization, ambient sound design, art pass. Break this into sub-tasks after M8 is validated — no point defining granular tasks before the core loop works.

issues.summary: Ship v0.3.0 — merge release PR #2981, tag, deploy to Cloud Run
issues.description:
Umbrella ticket for shipping v0.3.0 of tasteslikegood.org.
Release vehicle: Frontend PR #2981 "release(v0.3.0): align Backend submodule pin to valid dev tip 10910bc" — https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/pull/2981
Checklist:
Merge fix PR #2843 (TAS-8 — unblocks pytest, see TO-5)
Merge backend PR #120 "catch main up to dev" (backend main is stale; last backend tag is v0.1.11 vs frontend v0.2.5) — https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegood.com/pull/120
Verify Cloud Build tag-push trigger regex before tagging (see #2898 ticket)
Merge release PR #2981
Tag v0.3.0 on main (both repos per TO-1 convention)
Deploy flask-backend then express-frontend (Cloud Run, us-central1)
Smoke test: recipe generation, OAuth login, guest→OAuth migration
Publish GitHub Release with notes
Verify serving revisions match v0.3.0 tag (TO-1 alignment)
Related: TO-1, TO-5, TO-14. Track release scope with JQL: labels = Release-v0.3.0

issues.summary: [GH#2898] Verify Cloud Build tag-push trigger regex before tagging v0.3.0
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/2898
Verify the Cloud Build tag-push trigger regex matches ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ in project comdottasteslikegood.
Why release-critical: if the regex is wrong, pushing the v0.3.0 tag will not fire the build/deploy pipeline at all. Must be verified BEFORE tagging v0.3.0.
Verify with: gcloud builds triggers list --project comdottasteslikegood --format="table(name, triggerTemplate.tagName, github.push.tag)"
Deferred from v0.2.1.

issues.summary: [GH#2896] Reconcile .gcloudignore .git exclusion with cloudbuild.yaml submodule init
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/2896
.gcloudignore currently excludes .git, but cloudbuild.yaml runs submodule init — which needs .git present in the build context. Reconcile the two so the Backend submodule (tasteslikegood.com) actually gets pulled during Cloud Build.
Release relevance: the v0.3.0 release PR (#2981) is specifically about the backend submodule pin — if the build can't init the submodule, the deployed image won't contain the pinned backend.
Deferred from v0.2.1.

issues.summary: [GH#2897] Decide on .cloudbuildignore (absent — gcloud only reads .gcloudignore)
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/2897
.cloudbuildignore is currently absent — gcloud only reads .gcloudignore. Decide whether a .cloudbuildignore is needed (e.g., for trigger-based builds that use the GitHub source directly rather than gcloud upload) or document that .gcloudignore is the single source of truth.
Deferred from v0.2.1. Related: TO-38 (GH#2896).

issues.summary: [GH#2895] Fix solo-repo merge friction — ruleset requires review, auto-merge disabled
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/2895
The branch ruleset requires review approval but auto-merge is disabled — as a solo developer this creates merge friction and is a likely contributor to the current pile of open PRs (9 open in backend repo, ~20 in frontend incl. Dependabot).
Options to evaluate: enable auto-merge + allow self-bypass on the ruleset, use a merge queue, or relax the required-review rule for Dependabot/bot PRs while keeping it for feature branches.
Deferred from v0.2.1. Related: TO-15 (PR review practices).

issues.summary: [Backend GH#121] Fix broken Gemini workflows — triage 4 stacked draft PRs
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegood.com/issues/121
Repo: Backend submodule (tasteslikegood.com)
Workstream: TO-8 (DevOps & Cloud Infrastructure)
The Gemini GitHub workflows are broken. Four AI-generated draft PRs are stacked against this issue and need triage:
PR #122 — fix: add GEMINI_CLI_TRUST_WORKSPACE to all Gemini workflow steps (Copilot, 13/46 tasks)
PR #123 — [WIP] Fix Gemini workflows by addressing missing elements (Claude)
PR #124 — [WIP] Fix Gemini workflows to address missing components (Codex)
PR #136 — Fix CI signal reliability by aligning workflow scope and stabilizing lint/type checks (Copilot, 16/46 tasks)
Action: pick one approach, close the other drafts, merge. Not a v0.3.0 blocker but contributes to red CI noise.
